rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when diagnostics indicate scope mismatches.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference opaque Boolean or enumeration data types in numeric comparisons.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Ensure AGREE assumptions and guarantees are placed in the correct AADL component scope (type vs. implementation).
Remove AGREE clauses that reference invalid or inaccessible AADL features, especially those with type errors or scope mismatches.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code unless they are explicitly declared in the model's dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within AADL component types or implementations.
Implementation-level bindings in AGREE should only reference subcomponents and connections that are valid in the implementation scope.
Boolean or opaque data types in AGREE should not be used in numeric comparisons or range checks unless their scalar representation is explicitly defined.
anti_patterns: Do not generate AGREE assumptions or guarantees that reference identifiers not visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or variables.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric values for AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
